plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high risk.
Increase allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for stability and yield.
Trim broad index (SPY) and AI/Software (CRWD) to reduce concentration.
Add new position in Financials (XLF) for diversification.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing existing positions.
Focus on capital preservation with tight stops and lower overall leverage.
conservative.read: This plan prioritizes capital preservation, aiming for lower monthly gains. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration risk, accepting a slower growth rate. Drawdowns should be considerably smaller than current levels.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to stay within mandate limits.
Reduce concentration in SPY to meet the single name limit.
Slightly increase broad index exposure with QQQ.
Trim AI/Software (CRWD) to reduce single name concentration.
Add a high-scoring software name (PANW) for growth potential.
Balance growth and risk by blending index, quality tech, and limited leverage.
balanced.read: This balanced approach seeks moderate growth while managing risk. It reduces concentration and leverage, incorporating strong new setups. Expect steady gains with controlled drawdowns, a step up from conservative but below aggressive.

plan: Increase allocation to leveraged ETF (SOXL) for aggressive growth.
Eliminate the highest risk leveraged ETF (SPAL).
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce concentration and fund new adds.
Increase conviction in strong AI/Software (CRWD) and add NVDA.
Add a high-scoring short-bias ETF (TECS) for tactical plays.
Concentrate capital in highest conviction, highest scoring names and leverage.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan aims for high growth by increasing leverage and concentration in top-rated setups. Expect significantly higher potential gains but also substantially larger drawdowns and leverage decay risk. Strict stop discipline is critical.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate volatility.
Maximize allocation to income ETF (QQQI) for steady distributions.
Trim broad index (SPY, QQQ) to reduce growth focus and increase cash.
Sell high-beta single names (CRWD) and volatile sectors (SMH) to cut risk.
Prioritize capital preservation and reliable monthly income over growth.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for stability and yield.
income.read: This income-focused plan prioritizes stable monthly distributions and capital preservation. It shifts heavily into income-generating assets and cuts all high-risk, high-beta positions. Expect low drawdowns and consistent, though variable, income, with minimal capital appreciation.
